perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated by either party by giving thirty (30) days notice in writing to the other party without assigning any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated by either party by giving thirty (30) days notice in writing to the other party; however, termination is only permitted if there is a material breach of contract.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement enables either party to terminate without cause. The modified agreement states termination can only occur in the event of a material breach of contract. This introduces an in-text contradiction relating to the conditions needed to terminate the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain valid for a period of three (3) years from the date of execution of this Agreement which term may be extended by mutual consent in writing of both the parties. This Agreement may be terminated by either party by giving thirty (30) days notice in writing to the other party; however, termination is only permitted if there is a material breach of contract. The obligations of each party hereunder will continue and be binding irrespective of whether the discussion between the parties materialise into a specific understanding/business relationship or not and for a further period of three (3) years after termination/expiry of the Agreement. In any event, however, the obligations of each Party to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information it has received under this agreement shall continue indefinitely as to Confidential Information constituting a trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows termination without cause, while the changed text limits termination to cases of material breach. The contradicted text includes the modified clause, which creates an in-text contradiction, since the agreement cannot be terminated freely, due to the change. The change causes a conflict between the two conditions for termination, and therefore constitutes an in-text contradiction, as described in the perturbation explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information coming to the knowledge of the other may relate to and/or have implications regarding the future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes and or information of the parties, which afford them certain competitive and strategic advantage. Accordingly neither party shall use the Confidential Information in a manner that will jeopardise or adversely affect in any manner such future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes, information, and/or competitive and strategic advantage of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information coming to the knowledge of the other may relate to and/or have implications regarding the future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes and or information of the parties, which afford them certain competitive and strategic advantage. Either party may use the Confidential Information in their business activities, as long as its used for business development purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that neither party can use the confidential information in a manner that will jeopardise or adversely affect in any manner such future strategies, plans, business activities. The changed text allows the parties to use the Confidential Information in their business activities, as long as its used for business development purposes. This creates an in-text contradiction as it changes how to Confidential Information can be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information coming to the knowledge of the other may relate to and/or have implications regarding the future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes and or information of the parties, which afford them certain competitive and strategic advantage. Either party may use the Confidential Information in their business activities, as long as its used for business development purposes. 9. The parties hereto acknowledge and agree that in the event of a breach or threatened breach by the other of the provisions of this Agreement, the party not in breach will have no adequate remedy in money or damages and accordingly the party not in breach shall be entitled to injunctive relief against such breach or threatened breach by the party in breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither party can use the confidential information in a manner that will jeopardise or adversely affect in any manner such future strategies, plans, business activities. The changed text allows the parties to use the Confidential Information in their business activities, as long as its used for business development purposes, which directly contradicts the original restriction. The contradicted text is simply a duplicate of the changed text and also includes a clause about breaching provisions. This shows that the new text contradicts the original purpose of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Each party will bear its own costs in connection with the activities undertaken in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party will bear its own costs in connection with the activities undertaken in connection with this Agreement, except that BCGI shall reimburse XIUS for all reasonable travel expenses incurred by XIUS in connection with negotiation of the possible business relationship.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states each party will bear its own costs but the changed agreement introduces an exception stating that BCGI shall reimburse XIUS for all reasonable travel expenses. This contradicts which party is responsible for its cost.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party will bear its own costs in connection with the activities undertaken in connection with this Agreement, except that BCGI shall reimburse XIUS for all reasonable travel expenses incurred by XIUS in connection with negotiation of the possible business relationship. 16. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to confer any rights/remedies under or by reason of this Agreement on any third party. 17. This Agreement supersedes all prior discussions and writings with respect to the Confidential Information and constitutes the entire Agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. If any term or provision of this Agreement is determined to be illegal, unenforceable, or invalid in whole or in part for any reason, such illegal, unenforceable, or invalid provisions or part(s) thereof shall be stricken from this Agreement and such provision shall not affect the legality, enforceability, or validity of the remainder of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Each party will bear its own costs'. The changed text adds an exception: 'except that BCGI shall reimburse XIUS for all reasonable travel expenses'. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The changed text directly contradicts the original statement of each party bearing its own cost, by explicitly stating that BCGI will reimburse XIUS for travel expenses. Therefore, the modified text creates a direct conflict and an in-text contradiction.
